title: Reveals, Notches, Liquid Type, Peel & Native Scroll
description: Scroll-timeline word reveals, computed non-rectangular bento shapes, an SVG liquid-glass text lens, a drag-to-peel sticker, and a native zero-JS CSS carousel.

drops.title: Word Reveal
drops.description: Scroll drives a word-by-word text highlight with zero JavaScript animation logic.
drops.concept: Each paragraph gets a named CSS view-timeline; every word span references that timeline with its own animation-range offset (staggered via a --wi custom property), so the words brighten in sequence purely as a function of scroll position through the block.
drops.source: Daniel Haim (CodePen) — featured in CodePen Radio's 'Trends of 2026 (So Far)'

interaction.mouse: scroll the page to sweep the reveal
interaction.click: swap the highlight color in the debug panel

drops.title: Notch Bento
drops.description: Bento cards clipped into scoops, notches, scallops and torn edges via runtime-computed SVG paths.
drops.concept: A vanilla-JS stand-in for CSS's upcoming border-shape/corner-shape property: each card's actual rendered size is measured, an SVG path (arcs, notches, scallops or a zigzag) is built to match, and applied via clip-path: path() plus filter: drop-shadow() so the shadow follows the real silhouette instead of the bounding box.
drops.source: Una Kravets — border-shape proposal, resurging via donotfold (CodePen)

interaction.mouse: hover cards; drag sliders to change radius/notch/scallop depth
interaction.touch: cards respond to tap; panel hidden on small screens

drops.title: Liquid Type
drops.description: A pointer-following glass lens bulges and chromatically fringes headline text.
drops.concept: Echoes Apple's Liquid Glass design language: a circular lens with backdrop-filter: url(#filter) follows the pointer over each headline. The SVG filter combines feTurbulence + feDisplacementMap for the glassy warp with an feOffset/feColorMatrix/feBlend chain that splits the red and blue channels for a chromatic-aberration fringe.

interaction.mouse: move across a headline to drag the lens
interaction.touch: drag on touch; releases into a gentle idle float

drops.title: Sticker Peel
drops.description: Hover curls a sticker's corner; dragging peels and tilts it before an elastic spring-back.
drops.concept: Three stacked layers (face + a flipped, darkened 'underside' layer) share a --peel custom property driving clip-path: inset(...). Hovering grows the peel slightly; dragging grows it further and adds a rotation proportional to drag distance for a tilt effect; releasing snaps peel, offset and tilt back to zero with a spring cubic-bezier transition.

interaction.mouse: hover to peek, drag to peel and tilt, release to spring back
interaction.click: press and hold to start a drag
interaction.touch: full drag support via Pointer Events

drops.title: Native Scroll Carousel
drops.description: Prev/next buttons and pagination dots generated entirely by CSS, with a JS fallback for older browsers.
drops.concept: Uses the new ::scroll-button(inline-start/inline-end) and ::scroll-marker/::scroll-marker-group pseudo-elements to get fully native, auto-disabling arrow buttons and an auto-highlighting dot strip on a scroll-snap container — zero JavaScript on supporting browsers. Feature-detected via CSS.supports(selector(...)) with a hand-built JS carousel (buttons + IntersectionObserver-style nearest-card detection) as the fallback.
drops.source: Jo Mändle & Chrome for Developers — Native CSS Carousels

interaction.mouse: click arrow buttons or dots, or drag-scroll
